stimulus: Researcher Linh Pham and colleagues showed that processing fees—the charges merchants must pay to accept card payments—reduce the appeal of accepting small purchases. Retailers can absorb these fees to encourage card use, but those fees, though variable, are largely ______blank transaction size, so retailers that absorb the fees will likely focus on attracting a few large purchases over many small ones.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "Unrelated to" most logically completes the text. Because processing fees do not scale meaningfully with transaction size, retailers can dilute the fixed cost of a fee over a larger purchase—so they prefer large purchases. This only follows if the fee is roughly unrelated to size.

Choice A is incorrect. If fees were predictive of size, they would scale with size, and large purchases would carry similarly large fees—undermining the conclusion. Choice C is incorrect. "Absorbed by" doesn't fit grammatically or semantically with the rest of the sentence. Choice D is incorrect. "Additive to" is awkward and does not explain why retailers would favor large transactions.
